calendar.matters:
Petition For Approval Of Resignation Of Trustee; And Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-15-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Aspelin for Rolf Seebach. Albert Kun for and with Connie Maratas.

The Court informed Mr. Kun that a verification was presented to the Examiner, that it doesn't have a cover, and that documents without a cover do not make it into the file. Mr. Kun voir dired his client, under penalty of perjury, as to the verification. Ms. Maratas confirmed the verification. The Court stated that Mr. Seeback wished to resign. Mr. Aspelin argued that the objections as written don't make sense, that there has been a long history here, and that there is no reason for the objection. Mr. Kun presented argument, stated that there are two homes in Sebastapol, that the home where the two disabled Sons reside has an attorney lien, that they want to brief the issue, and that they are requesting a hearing. Mr. Aspelin stated that before there would be any action by the Trustee with regard to the property that they would receive ample notice. Mr. Kun argued that this was not in the best interest of the Trust or the Court's time, that Beatrice is willing to serve without fees, that his client doesn't want anything, that she is here to protect the interest of her two Brothers, and that the matter should be set for hearing. Mr. Aspelin stated that they object to Beatrice being appointed for the reasons set forth on the record. At the conclusion of argument, the Court granted the Petition for Approval of Trustee, and to Appoint Successor Trustee (subject to review of the order), and denied Mr. Kun's requests for further briefing and to set the matter for hearing.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-03-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for and with Connie Maratas. John Aspelin for Rolf Seebach. Sammy Maratas was also present.

Mr. Kun expressed what the Trustor's intent was. Mr. Aspelin stated that he revised the proposed order, that he was not putting a lien on the third property, spoke of the property description and stated that he spoke with Mr. Seebach regarding the need to keep time records. The matter was taken under submission for review of the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-06-2010 Continued To Oct-20-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Albert Kun for and with Connie Maratas. John Aspelin for Rolf Seebach.

Mr. Kun stated that he is in a jury trial in Department 604, that it is expected to go through 10/15/10, and that he is requesting a continuance to 10/20/10. Mr. Aspelin stated that he had just spoken with Mr. Kun on Friday, that he hasn't reached his client yet, that there is no reason for the continuance, and that he is ready to go forward with the accounting hearing. Mr. Kun addressed being new to the case. Discussions regarding the real property were held. Mr. Aspelin stated that they would designate the rental property as the property to place the lien against and that he hasn't decided which property it will be. The Court stated in regard to the lien that he must indicate a specific property and that it must include the legal description of the property. The Court also addressed the issue of the guideline fees. The Court stated in regard to the previous one-percent that was allowed that in the future that Mr. Seebach may wish to keep track of his time because most of the litigation difficulties were behind them. The Court continued the matter to October 20, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow time for Mr. Kun to review the paperwork since he is new to the case, and requested that he inform the Court and Mr. Aspelin if he has any objections to the second account noting that it understood that he is currently in trial.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-13-2010 Continued To Oct-06-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-16-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Aspelin for and with Rolf Seebach. Connie Maratas (Objector) in pro per. Beatrice Gardner (Objector) in pro per. Sammy Maratas (Objector) in pro per. Ted Maratas (Objecter) in pro per.

Mr. Aspelin stated that he filed the verification. The Court informed Connie Maratas that she filed objections on behalf of her entire family but since she is not an attorney she can only file on behalf of herself. Mr. Aspelin presented argument stating that Mr. Seebach took over when things were in disarray and that all acts were necessary. Connie Maratas presented argument stating that Mr. Seebach didn't have to sell the house he just had to sign papers, that she handed all documentation over to him, that she failed to keep copies for herself, that she requested copies of the documents but they weren't given to her, and that they don't want to pay him more than $50.00 an hour. Ted Maratas presented his concerns for the record stating that Mr. Seebach is harassing them, causing dissension, and working against them. Sammy Maratas stated that he trusted Mr. Seebach with his taxes, that Mr. Seebach took them to his CPA, that it cost him $500.00 which could have been done for less, that the rents were collected to help him, and that now he may have to pay that money back. Ms. Gardner stated that it seems as if Mr. Seebach has his own agenda and that they want him to resign. Mr. Aspelin responded stating that Mr. Seebach acted appropriately, that he had to proceed as to the terms of the Trust, and that the issue of removal was not before the Court. At the conclusion of argument, the matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Court Reporter: Nici Rodich, CSR #11604. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Allowance Of Fees And Costs To Attorneys
Off Calendar Mar-08-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder in Propria Persona. John Aspelin for Rolf Seebach (Trustee). Connie Maratas in Propria Persona.

Mr. Feder explained the extent of services provided to support his fee request. Mr. Aspelin and Ms. Maratas presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the accountings and fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-23-2010 Continued To Mar-30-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-12-2010 Continued To Feb-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Continued To Jan-12-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for/with Connie Maratas (Former Trustee). Andy Tran also for and with Connie Maratas. John Aspelin for and with Rolf Seebach (Trustee).

Mr. Feder addressed the value of the real property stating that the accountant who prepared the state taxes later changed the value on the 706 to a smaller amount; the correct amount. Mr. Feder then addressed the rental income stating that it is money that went to two disabled Brothers, that it was agreed amongst the siblings that the rental income would go to them, that this is what their Mother would have wanted, and that it would be easy enough to adjust this as an advance at the time of distribution. Mr. Aspelin argued that Eva did not agree. Mr. Feder stated that he had a letter from 2005 from Eva stating that she was ok with the money going to the disabled Brothers but not ok with the expenses for the rental property coming from her portion. The Court inquired about the expenses. Mr. Feder stated that some were paid by the Brothers and some were paid by the Trust and that they can figure that out. Mr. Aspelin argued that they haven't had an accouting since January 2009 and stated that they agreed they would have to come back to get distributions. Mr. Feder stated that there wasn't co-mingling of funds, addressed the Totten Trusts/need to pay taxes, stated that the siblings had to obtain money outside the Trust to pay the taxes, that some siblings wrote Ms. Moratas a check, and that some deposited the money directly. In addition, he stated the only money in her account is the $10,000.00 refund and that there is no problem in paying the $10,000.00. The Court stated that the $10,000.00 should go into the Trust. Mr. Feder stated that the Trust administration has been difficult and that his client did what she thought her Mother wanted. Additional argument by Mr. Aspelin. Matter submitted.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-26-2009 Continued To Nov-30-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-26-2009 Continued To Nov-30-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-17-2009 Continued To Oct-26-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Connie Maratas. Andy Tran for and with Connie Maratas. John Aspelin for and with Rolf Seebach.

The Court stated that there were two petitions pending but it was only dealing with Connie Maratas' First Account today. Mr. Feder stated that there was an issue with a bank officer, that his client was unable to obtain the bank statements, and that he was out of town when this issue came up but they could try again. In addition, Mr. Feder addressed the tax issue stating that the Federal taxes were paid, that some payments were paid directly to the Federal Government; some of the beneficiaries wrote checks directly to the Federal Government, and the Estate tax was paid by someone who didn't owe it and was then reimbursed. Mr. Aspelin stated that they were back to where they started, that they weren't making any progress, that it looked like there wouldn't be an accounting any time soon, that he would be willing to sit down with the parties, and that his client is requesting a CPA be present for the meeting. Mr. Feder responded stating that they had filed an accounting, that a substantial amount of money has been accounted for, and that what they have issues with had to do with activities regarding funds not held in the Trust. Additional arguments were held regarding co-mingling of of Trust and non-Trust assets. Mr. Aspelin suggested the matters be continued sixty days for the parties to meet. The Court continued the matters to October 26, 2009, and stated that it would leave it up to Mr. Feder as to whether or not a CPA would be present at the meeting.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Murphy.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Former Trustee'S Account
Off Calendar Dec-30-2008 Continued To Jan-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Connie Maratas (Former Trustee). John Aspelin and James Bridgman for Rolf Seebach (Current Trustee).

Mr. Feder reported that they were still working on the accounting, that all of the records had been turned over to Mr. Seebach, that Mr. Seebach returned some of the records but that some were still missing. In addition, he stated that they subpoenaed the banks, but that Washington Mutual and U. S. Bank had not provided the requested documents and that they had made all efforts to obtain these records. Mr. Aspelin stated that they were entitled to sanctions and entitled to their fees and costs. The Court denied Mr. Aspelin's request for sanctions without prejudice. The matter was continued to January 27, 2009 for further status. The hearing on the Former Trustee's accounting and the hearing on the Current Trustee's accounting were continued to March 3, 2009.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Leticia Perez, CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition To Compel Predecessor Trustees To Account
Off Calendar Oct-22-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Aspelin for and with Rolf Seebach. Bruce Feder for and with Connie Maratas (Former Trustee).

Mr. Aspelin stated that the accounting had not been filed, that Mr. Feder had ample time to determine what was needed, that he wanted to know who the accountant was and that he wanted copies of the paid taxes. In addition, Mr. Aspelin requested the accounting be provided 3 weeks prior to the hearing date. Mr. Feder stated that they were actively working on the accounting, that they were missing many bank statements, that he needed to subpeona the bank for the records and requested a continuance. Mr. Feder informed Mr. Aspelin that the accountant was Robbin Russell. The Court granted the petition, (subject to review of the order). Hearing on the Former Trustee's account was set for January 27, 2009. Status on filing of the accounting was set for December 30, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-26-2008 Continued To Oct-28-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Predecessor Trustees To Account
Off Calendar Aug-26-2008 Continued To Oct-21-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Connie Maratas. John Aspelin for Rolf Seebach. Eva Mulky also present. Beatrice Gardner also present.

Mr. Feder stated that the accounting was being worked on, that all the records had been turned over to the Successor and that he hoped to complete and file the accounting within 45 days. The matter was continued to October 21, 2008. Mr. Feder's accounting must be filed by that date. Mr. Aspelin's petition was continued to October 28, 2008. Mr. Feder will review information provided by Eva Mulky and Beatrice Gardner in regards to their handling of trust assets. If there are no objections to the accountings, no appearance is needed on those dates.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.
